groundtruth:
In general,
\[\frac{1}{2} \begin{vmatrix} x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1 \end{vmatrix}\]is the signed area of the triangle with vertices at $(x_1,y_1),$ $(x_2,y_2),$ and $(x_3,y_3).$  (The area is signed, i.e. it can be positive or negative, depending on the orientation of the triangle.)  Here, the sides of the triangle are 3, 4, and 5, which is a right triangle.  Therefore, its area is $\frac{1}{2} \cdot 3 \cdot 4 = 6.$  Then
\[\begin{vmatrix} x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1 \end{vmatrix} = \pm 12,\]so
\[\begin{vmatrix} x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1 \end{vmatrix}^2 = \boxed{144}.\]

output.text:
To find \(\begin{vmatrix} x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\ x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\ x_3 & y_3 & 1 \end{vmatrix}^2\), we first need to compute the determinant of the given matrix and then square it. ки
The matrix is:
\[
\begin{pmatrix}
x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\
x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\
x_3 & y_3 & 1
\end{pmatrix}
\] ки
The determinant of this matrix is:
\[
\begin{vmatrix}
x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\
x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\
x_3 & y_3 & 1
\end{vmatrix} = x_1(y_2 - y_3) + y_1(x_2 - x_3) + 1(x_3 - x_2)
\] ки
We can simplify this expression:
\[
x_1(y_2 - y_3) + y_1(x_2 - x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) = x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3 + y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3 + x_3 - x_2
\] ки
Rearranging terms, we get:
\[
x_1 y_2 + y_1 x_2 - x_1 y_3 - y_1 x_3 + x_3 - x_2
\] ки
We can group the terms involving \(x_1\) and \(y_1\) separately:
\[
x_1(y_2 - y_3) + y_1(x_2 - x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) = (x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3) + (y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3) + (x_3 - x_2)
\] ки
Notice that:
\[
(x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3) + (y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) = x_1(y_2 - y_3) + y_1(x_2 - x_3) + x_3 - x_2
\] ки
We can see that the expression simplifies to:
\[
x_1(y_2 - y_3) + y_1(x_2 - x_3) + x_3 - x_2 = x_1(y_2 - y_3) + y_1(x_2 - x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) = (x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3) + (y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3) + (x_3 - x_2)
\] ки
We can see that this expression is symmetric and does not depend on the specific values of \(x_i\) and \(y_i\). Instead, we can use the given conditions to find the value of the determinant squared. ки
Given:
\[
(x_1 - x_2)^2 + (y_1 - y_2)^2 = 9,
\]
\[
(x_1 - x_3)^2 + (y_1 - y_3)^2 = 16,
\]
\[
(x_2 - x_3)^2 + (y_2 - y_3)^2 = 25.
\] ки
We can use the identity for the square of the determinant of the matrix:
\[
\left( \begin{vmatrix}
x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\
x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\
x_3 & y_3 & 1
\end{vmatrix} \right)^2 = \left( \sum_{i=1}^3 \left( x_i y_j + y_i x_j \right) \right)^2
\] ки
We can expand this to:
\[
\left( \sum_{i=1}^3 \left( x_i y_j + y_i x_j \right) \right)^2 = \left( \sum_{i=1}^3 x_i y_i + \sum_{i=1}^3 y_i x_i \right)^2 = \left( 2 \sum_{i=1}^3 x_i y_i \right)^2
\] ки
Using the given conditions, we can find:
\[
\sum_{i=1}^3 x_i y_i = \frac{1}{2} \left( (x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3) + (y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) \right)
\] ки
We can see that:
\[
\sum_{i=1}^3 x_i y_i = \frac{1}{2} \left( (x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3) + (y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) \right)
\] ки
Therefore:
\[
\left( \begin{vmatrix}
x_1 & y_1 & 1 \\
x_2 & y_2 & 1 \\
x_3 & y_3 & 1
\end{vmatrix} \right)^2 = \left( \frac{1}{2} \left( (x_1 y_2 - x_1 y_3) + (y_1 x_2 - y_1 x_3) + (x_3 - x_2) \right) \right)^2 = \boxed{144} ки
